label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking why smog has become a common concern. The options are A, B, C, D. First, I'll look back at the passage. The relevant part is in the third paragraph: "As a popular online post said, air may be the only thing that is equal for everyone, despite your income or profession. People with higher incomes are able to drink only bottled spring water and eat only organic food by paying higher prices, but they breathe the same air as everyone else." So the key point here is that no matter your income or job, everyone has to breathe the same air. So smog affects everyone equally. That's why it's a common concern. It's not something you can avoid by paying more, unlike water or food. So the answer is probably D, because nobody can avoid it. Let me check the options again. A is about paying higher prices, but the passage says people can pay for better water and food but not air. B is because a popular online post discussed it. While the post is mentioned, the actual reason given is the equality of exposure. C is about developing industry, but
